content.full_text:
FY80 8

[signature]

A-76-332

June 2, 1976


Honorable Newton R. Russell
Senator, 21st District
State Capitol
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Senator Russell:

This letter is in response to your inquiry of May 24, 1976
concerning disclosure requirements of the Political Reform
Act.

You are not required to report reimbursement for your out-
of-pocket expenses in connection with representing the
Republican Party at the cocktail party. By attending the
function, you provided equal consideration for the amount you
received as reimbursement for your dinner expense.

If we can provide further assistance, please contact our staff.

Sincerely,


NAN HAMBLETON
Special Compliance Representative


NH:mak
bcc: Jeanne

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
95814

DISTRICT OFFICE
818 SOUTH CENTRAL
GLENDALE, CALIFORNIA 91204
TELEPHONE: (213) 247-7021

NEWTON R. RUSSELL
TWENTY-FIRST SENATORIAL DISTRICT

CALIFORNIA LEGISLATURE

COMMITTEES:
BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS
EMPLOYMENT AND RETIREMENT
INSURANCE AND FINANCIAL
INSTITUTIONS
INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS
PUBLIC UTILITIES, TRANSIT
AND ENERGY
JOINT COMMITTEE:
EDUCATIONAL GOALS AND
EVALUATION
MOTOR VEHICLE INSPECTIONS
SELECT COMMITTEES:
CHILDREN AND YOUTH
VEHICLE DESIGN AND PERFORMANCE
SENATE SUBCOMMITTEE ON
SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA RAPID
TRANSIT

Senate

May 24, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

On May 6 I attended a cocktail party for Bruce Makar,
Republican Candidate for State Senate, 6th District.

The cocktail party was from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. in Hillsborough.
My wife and I were asked to attend to represent the official
Republican Party. We stopped for dinner before returning to
Sacramento. I expended $31.00 and have been reimbursed by
the Senate Republican Caucus for this out-of-pocket expense.

Does this need to be reported in any fashion? If so, please
advise as to how this should be handled.

If I hear nothing further, I will assume that this is a simple reimbursement of expenses and need not be reported.

Sincerely,

NEWTON R. RUSSELL
Senator, 21st District

NRR:ic
IV-D

sections.question: On May 6 I attended a cocktail party for Bruce Makar, Republican Candidate for State Senate, 6th District. The cocktail party was from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. in Hillsborough. My wife and I were asked to attend to represent the official Republican Party. We stopped for dinner before returning to Sacramento. I expended $31.00 and have been reimbursed by the Senate Republican Caucus for this out-of-pocket expense. Does this need to be reported in any fashion? If so, please advise as to how this should be handled.
sections.conclusion: You are not required to report reimbursement for your out-of-pocket expenses in connection with representing the Republican Party at the cocktail party. By attending the event, if you provided equal consideration for the amount you received as reimbursement for your dinner expense.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a state senator report a $31.00 reimbursement from the Senate Republican Caucus for dinner expenses incurred while attending a cocktail party for a Republican candidate in an official party capacity?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the senator is not required to report the reimbursement for dinner expenses incurred while representing the Republican Party at the event, provided equal consideration was given for the reimbursed amount.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (May 24, 1976 letter from Senator Russell) and FPPC response (June 2, 1976 letter from Nan Hew/Bleton). Conclusion section is partially garbled/unclear in original document ('By attending the f you provided equal consideration'). Minor OCR/transcription errors present in source material.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: On May 6 I attended a cocktail party for Bruce Makar, Republican Candidate for State Senate, 6th District. The cocktail party was from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. in Hillsborough. My wife and I were asked to attend to represent the official Republican Party. We stopped for dinner before returning to Sacramento. I expended $31.00 and have been reimbursed by the Senate Republican Caucus for this out-of-pocket expense. Does this need to be reported in any fashion? If so, please advise as to how this should be handled.

CONCLUSION: You are not required to report reimbursement for your out-of-pocket expenses in connection with representing the Republican Party at the cocktail party. By attending the event, if you provided equal consideration for the amount you received as reimbursement for your dinner expense.

embedding.summary: Senator Newton R. Russell inquired whether a $31.00 reimbursement from the Senate Republican Caucus for dinner expenses while attending a campaign cocktail party in an official party capacity requires disclosure under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC advised that no reporting is required for this reimbursement.
